statement.en: Paper 3: The Holographic Principle and Surface-Area Scaling in a Holosphere Lattice
statement.summary: We present an operational, testable interpretation of holography in Holosphere Theory in which long-lived information is accounted for as a boundary defect ledger. The surface-area scaling of the defect inventory is taken as an upstream result (Paper A), with thermodynamic normalization delegated to Papers C and D. We model black holes as VROS-Bounded Coherence Shells (VBCS): coherence boundaries supported by the VROS scaffold (Paper J) rather than reflective material surfaces. In this transport-first framework, gravity does not require a persistent emission flux from the compact object; it arises from spatial contrast in admissible transport, summarized by a bounded coherence-potential gradient (Papers 19, 24, 62). We introduce and validate a residual-only near-horizon imaging diagnostic

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper presents novel theoretical framework (Holosphere Theory, VBCS model) with claimed testability but lacks peer validation, experimental confirmation, or published validation chain. Status explicitly provisional (2026 preprint). Core claims remain unvalidated: surface-area scaling derivation delegated to upstream work, thermodynamic normalization to downstream papers, diagnostic validation incomplete ('introduce and validate' is truncated). Coherence-shell gravity model contradicts established GR without demonstrated empirical advantage.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 境界の欠陥が面積に従い、それが内部の流れのポテンシャル勾配を織り込む。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 表層の欠陥スケジョグが内部の流れ対称性を支える、二重構造の膜。
